name: Alford, Neil Herbert
motto: The enemy of art is the enemy of nature; art is nothing but the highest sagacity and exertion of human nature; and what will he honour who honours not the human?
description: Received his early education in Marion Graded School. Doomed; upon his last legs, cotton lint, sallow faces; forever a yap.
text:
The enemy of art is the enemy of nature; art is nothing but the highest sagacity and exertion of human nature; and what will he honour who honours not the human?
Received his early education in Marion Graded School. Doomed; upon his last legs, cotton lint, sallow faces; forever a yap.

name: Black, William Edward Gregg
motto: They are never alone who are accompanied with noble thoughts.
clubs: Palmetto Literary Society (Recording Secretary, Literary Critic, President)
description: Will be a great railroad man—a Blue Ridge flagman.
text:
They are never alone who are accompanied with noble thoughts.
Will be a great railroad man—a Blue Ridge flagman.

name: Barnwell, William Habersham
motto: Tax not so bad a voice to slander music any more at once.
description: Received early education at Graded Schools in Louisville, Ky., and returned to South Carolina in 1886. Will see high life in the Vaudeville shows, as a ragtime singer.
text:
Tax not so bad a voice to slander music any more at once.
Received early education at Graded Schools in Louisville, Ky., and returned to South Carolina in 1886. Will see high life in the Vaudeville shows, as a ragtime singer.

name: Beaty, James Thornwell
motto: It is a good thing to laugh at any rate; and if a straw can tickle a man, it is an instrument of happiness.
description: Received his early education at Mt. Zion Institute. Seeking a mechanical course he entered the Freshman Class at Clemson in 1899 and pursued the Civil Engineering Course. A great engineer he will be, and tunnel through the earth to the other side.
text:
It is a good thing to laugh at any rate; and if a straw can tickle a man, it is an instrument of happiness.
Received his early education at Mt. Zion Institute. Seeking a mechanical course he entered the Freshman Class at Clemson in 1899 and pursued the Civil Engineering Course. A great engineer he will be, and tunnel through the earth to the other side.

name: Bradford, Jules Lagrave
motto: "A man has not enough range of thought to look out for any good which does not relate to his own interest."
description: Received his early education at Palmetto Collegiate Institute at Lexington and Haywood's Academy at Columbia, S.C. He will cause nations to stampede, and the earth to tremble.
text:
"A man has not enough range of thought to look out for any good which does not relate to his own interest."
Received his early education at Palmetto Collegiate Institute at Lexington and Haywood's Academy at Columbia, S.C. He will cause nations to stampede, and the earth to tremble.

name: Boineau, Charles Evans
motto: Lend me thy meal ticket, I conjure thee! Lend, by the sacred name of friendship.
description: Received his early education at the Beaufort and Allendale Public Schools. Will be a "news butcher".
text:
Lend me thy meal ticket, I conjure thee! Lend, by the sacred name of friendship.
Received his early education at the Beaufort and Allendale Public Schools. Will be a "news butcher".

name: Cain, William Odell Jr.
motto: Never too late to do better.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Y. M. C. A.
Delegate, Y. M. C. A. to Greenwood, Sumter and Columbia
Delegate, Southern Students Conference in Asheville, N. C.
description: Received his early education at Ramsey, S. C., and at Wedgefield Graded School, and then attended S. C. C. I. In his Senior year was Lieutenant Co. F. Textile Course. We will hear of him as a "Man of Letters"—a mail carrier.
text:
Never too late to do better.
Received his early education at Ramsey, S. C., and at Wedgefield Graded School, and then attended S. C. C. I. In his Senior year was Lieutenant Co. F. Textile Course. We will hear of him as a "Man of Letters"—a mail carrier.

name: Cummings, Joseph Presley
motto: Give him time and he will say something.
description: Received his early education at Mt. Zion Institute and Columbia Graded School. Early in life his good looks will win for him a wife in the "Smart Set."
text:
Give him time and he will say something.
Received his early education at Mt. Zion Institute and Columbia Graded School. Early in life his good looks will win for him a wife in the "Smart Set."

name: Cullum, James Colwell Jr.
motto: The fellow who his narrow bed had kept, Was weary, and without a rocker slept.
description: Buck was born August 5th, 1882, at Batesburg, S. C. Received his early education in Batesburg Institute. Member of Columbian Literary Society, and Epicurean and Tennis Clubs. Textile Course. Ten years will find him a jolly drummer; a seller of dyes, and a dealer in lyes.
text:
The fellow who his narrow bed had kept, Was weary, and without a rocker slept.
Buck was born August 5th, 1882, at Batesburg, S. C. Received his early education in Batesburg Institute. Member of Columbian Literary Society, and Epicurean and Tennis Clubs. Textile Course. Ten years will find him a jolly drummer; a seller of dyes, and a dealer in lyes.

name: Chisolm, William B.
motto: Do then as your progenitors have done, And by their virtues prove yourself their son.
clubs: Associate Art Editor, staff of Oconeean
description: Will lead the life of a Crook, for he walks in a crooked way.
text:
Do then as your progenitors have done, And by their virtues prove yourself their son.
Will lead the life of a Crook, for he walks in a crooked way.

name: Cunningham, Joseph Gilbert
motto: "Let scribes and heroes both of blasphemy beware; But if they must rip out an oath, by gad, then, let 'em swear."
description: Joe was born July 19th, 1882, at Anderson, S. C. Received his early education at the public and private schools of Anderson. Entered Clemson as "Fresh." September, 1899; he was a member of the band, orchestra, Glee Club, Tennis and German Clubs. Joe took the Textile Course and hopes some day to excel in dyeing. He will leg it out, for, as "Weary Willy," he'll find no rest.
text:
"Let scribes and heroes both of blasphemy beware; But if they must rip out an oath, by gad, then, let 'em swear."
Joe was born July 19th, 1882, at Anderson, S. C. Received his early education at the public and private schools of Anderson. Entered Clemson as "Fresh." September, 1899; he was a member of the band, orchestra, Glee Club, Tennis and German Clubs. Joe took the Textile Course and hopes some day to excel in dyeing. He will leg it out, for, as "Weary Willy," he'll find no rest.

name: Cunningham, Frank Harrison
motto: Music hath an impressive as well as an expressive, power and purpose.
description: He obtained his early education from the various schools of that city. In September, 1899 he came to Clemson, and joined in the "scrap" for a "sheepskin" and learning. Frank was elected to the band during his Freshman year and has held the position from that time. In September, 1902, he was elected leader of the band and orchestra, of which he is still a member; a member of the German, Tennis and Glee Clubs; sang bass in Glee Club and class quartet; Art Editor on staff of Oconeean; Chairman Music Hop Committee, 1902. A court crier, and vegetable vendor in the town of Anderson.
text:
Music hath an impressive as well as an expressive, power and purpose.
He obtained his early education from the various schools of that city. In September, 1899 he came to Clemson, and joined in the "scrap" for a "sheepskin" and learning. Frank was elected to the band during his Freshman year and has held the position from that time. In September, 1902, he was elected leader of the band and orchestra, of which he is still a member; a member of the German, Tennis and Glee Clubs; sang bass in Glee Club and class quartet; Art Editor on staff of Oconeean; Chairman Music Hop Committee, 1902. A court crier, and vegetable vendor in the town of Anderson.

name: Drayton, Edwin Earle
motto: "I thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated to closeness, and the bettering of my mind."
description: Received his early education at Central, S. C. Entered Furman University, but decided that Clemson was the college to get a textile education and joined the Class of '03 in September, 1900; member of the Columbian Literary Society and barrack mail carrier during his Senior year; Lieutenant in Co. "G." Poor fellow; life as a spinner of yarns.
text:
"I thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated to closeness, and the bettering of my mind."
Received his early education at Central, S. C. Entered Furman University, but decided that Clemson was the college to get a textile education and joined the Class of '03 in September, 1900; member of the Columbian Literary Society and barrack mail carrier during his Senior year; Lieutenant in Co. "G." Poor fellow; life as a spinner of yarns.

name: Ellis, Ebenezer Dunn
motto: We are not solicitous of the opinions and censures of men, but only that we do our duty.
hometown: near Donalds, Abbeville County, South Carolina
clubs: Palmetto Literary Society (Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer)
Delegate, Y.M.C.A. Convention at Columbia, South Carolina
Delegate, Southern Students Conference at Asheville, North Carolina
description: Received his early education at Due West, South Carolina. Will do the stunt on the "Variety Stage"—tragedy is in his eye.
text:
We are not solicitous of the opinions and censures of men, but only that we do our duty.
Received his early education at Due West, South Carolina. Will do the stunt on the "Variety Stage"—tragedy is in his eye.

name: DeSaussure, Fredrick Green
motto: The charms of poetry our souls bewitch; The curse of writing is an endless itch.
description: Fred has a love for both the beautiful and ugly in life. Will write poetry as a pastime, and for his friends to read.
text:
The charms of poetry our souls bewitch; The curse of writing is an endless itch.
Fred has a love for both the beautiful and ugly in life. Will write poetry as a pastime, and for his friends to read.

name: Fox, Lewis Wigfall
motto: Meantime he smokes, and laughs at merry tales, A pun ambiguous, or conundrum quaint.
description: Received early education at Batesburg Graded School. Afterwards entered Clemson; member of Columbian Literary Society, in which he held the following offices: Corresponding Secretary, Reporting Critic, Sergeant-at-Arms; member of Tennis, Epicurean, German and Glee Clubs; Lieutenant of Company B. Slyly he will creep into greatness and become a vineyard inspector.
text:
Meantime he smokes, and laughs at merry tales, A pun ambiguous, or conundrum quaint.
Received early education at Batesburg Graded School. Afterwards entered Clemson; member of Columbian Literary Society, in which he held the following offices: Corresponding Secretary, Reporting Critic, Sergeant-at-Arms; member of Tennis, Epicurean, German and Glee Clubs; Lieutenant of Company B. Slyly he will creep into greatness and become a vineyard inspector.

name: Epps, Silas Wightman
motto: Let me be no assistant for a State— But keep a farm and carters.
description: Received his early education at a county school near Kingstree. Being a lover of booze, we expect him to become his county dispenser.
text:
Let me be no assistant for a State— But keep a farm and carters.
Received his early education at a county school near Kingstree. Being a lover of booze, we expect him to become his county dispenser.

name: Finger, Edward R.
motto: His school hours are all the days and nights of his existence.
description: Received his early education in public schools in that county. In his Senior year held the office of Sergeant in the Artillery. Pursued the Civil Engineering Course. His greatness is in his perseverance. Will freeze to death in an attempt to survey the regions of the North Pole.
text:
His school hours are all the days and nights of his existence.
Received his early education in public schools in that county. In his Senior year held the office of Sergeant in the Artillery. Pursued the Civil Engineering Course. His greatness is in his perseverance. Will freeze to death in an attempt to survey the regions of the North Pole.

name: Freeman, Benjamin
motto: "A good book, an easy chair and a strong pipe are single pleasures, made one."
description: Received his early education in the Mount Pleasant Academy. Simply an asparagus grower.
text:
"A good book, an easy chair and a strong pipe are single pleasures, made one."
Received his early education in the Mount Pleasant Academy. Simply an asparagus grower.

name: Garrison, William David
motto: His fair large front and eye declare absolute rule.
hometown: Anderson County, near Denver, South Carolina
description: He will be feared as magistrate of Denver.
text:
His fair large front and eye declare absolute rule.
He will be feared as magistrate of Denver.

name: Gardner, Benjamin Hard
motto: The long day's task is done And we must now sleep.
clubs: Calhoun Literary Society (President, Secretary, Literary Critic)
description: Received his early education at Aiken Institute. Entered Clemson College September 1899; member of Calhoun Literary Society, holding the offices of President, Secretary, Literary Critic in that organization; won debater's medal contest, 1901l declaimer's medal. Untarnished now, but will rust on exposure, and become useless in life.
text:
The long day's task is done And we must now sleep.
Received his early education at Aiken Institute. Entered Clemson College September 1899; member of Calhoun Literary Society, holding the offices of President, Secretary, Literary Critic in that organization; won debater's medal contest, 1901l declaimer's medal. Untarnished now, but will rust on exposure, and become useless in life.

name: Gandy, Theodore S.
motto: "A barren spirited fellow; one that feeds on abject dits and imitations."
hometown: near Society Hill, Darlington Co., S. C.
description: Received his early education at Society Hill and Darlington Graded Schools. Salty, yet he yields no flavor; he will soon melt away.
text:
"A barren spirited fellow; one that feeds on abject dits and imitations."
Received his early education at Society Hill and Darlington Graded Schools. Salty, yet he yields no flavor; he will soon melt away.

name: Glenn, John Perry
motto: "The consciousness of duty performed gives us music at midnight."
description: Received his early education at Presbyterian College, Clinton, S. C., and at Fear Academy in Wilmington, N. C. Won declaimer's medal contest, 1900. Oration after oration he will deliver to the all-enduring public.
text:
"The consciousness of duty performed gives us music at midnight."
Received his early education at Presbyterian College, Clinton, S. C., and at Fear Academy in Wilmington, N. C. Won declaimer's medal contest, 1900. Oration after oration he will deliver to the all-enduring public.

name: Harry, Green
motto: Learn to hold thy tongue. Five words cost Zacarias forty weeks' silence.
description: A pie famine will ensue in 1910, then farewell to all that's green.
text:
Learn to hold thy tongue. Five words cost Zacarias forty weeks' silence.
A pie famine will ensue in 1910, then farewell to all that's green.

name: Hagood, Clarence Bruce
motto: My life is but a wind.
description: Received early education at Easley Graded School. A floor walker in a dry goods store will be this person's occupation.
text:
My life is but a wind.
Received early education at Easley Graded School. A floor walker in a dry goods store will be this person's occupation.

name: Harrall, J. Ernest
motto: Good company and good conversation are the sinews of virtue.
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms and Vice-President, Calhoun Literary Society
description: Top's originality of thought made him choice company. Will go as a missionary to India, and there marry a princess.
text:
Good company and good conversation are the sinews of virtue.
Top's originality of thought made him choice company. Will go as a missionary to India, and there marry a princess.

name: Harvey, Thomas Middleton
motto: There is no study that is not capable of delighting us after a little application to it.
description: Received his early education at Pinopolis Academy. He will reach the top (of a telegraph pole).
text:
There is no study that is not capable of delighting us after a little application to it.
Received his early education at Pinopolis Academy. He will reach the top (of a telegraph pole).

name: Haynesworth, Robert Elder
motto: However irregular and desultory in his talk, there is method in the fragments.
description: Received his early education in the Florence Graded Schools. A collector of cigarette butts, and a disposer of the same at half price.
text:
However irregular and desultory in his talk, there is method in the fragments.
Received his early education in the Florence Graded Schools. A collector of cigarette butts, and a disposer of the same at half price.

name: Humbert, David Glenn
motto: It is a small crime to wound himself by anguish of heart, to deprive himself of all the pleasures or cases, or enjoyments of life.
description: Will be shown in a dime museum.
text:
It is a small crime to wound himself by anguish of heart, to deprive himself of all the pleasures or cases, or enjoyments of life.
Will be shown in a dime museum.

name: Wayman, A. Holland
motto: It requires a critical nicety to find out the genius or the propensions of a child.
description: The inventor of our class will work in a toy factory.
text:
It requires a critical nicety to find out the genius or the propensions of a child.
The inventor of our class will work in a toy factory.

name: Jefferies, Samuel Lionel
motto: Economy is in itself a source of great revenue.
description: Received his early education at Campobello and High School, Spartanburg, S.C. Will be a specialist in hollow-horn and other similar diseases of cattle.
text:
Economy is in itself a source of great revenue.
Received his early education at Campobello and High School, Spartanburg, S.C. Will be a specialist in hollow-horn and other similar diseases of cattle.

name: Kaigler, Benjamin Hangabook
motto: Civility must be born, and never can be taught.
description: Received his early education at Pine Grove Academy and other private schools. Took a thorough course in low prep.; played on Freshman Class Football Team; was sub full-back in 1901 and left guard on Varsity team 1902. Will be popular as a lady's man and all-around man of the world.
text:
Civility must be born, and never can be taught.
Received his early education at Pine Grove Academy and other private schools. Took a thorough course in low prep.; played on Freshman Class Football Team; was sub full-back in 1901 and left guard on Varsity team 1902. Will be popular as a lady's man and all-around man of the world.

name: Berham, Hook Lawrence
motto: Man can effect no great matter by his personal strength, but as he acts in society and conjunction with others.
description: Received his early education at Darlington Graded Schools. Member of the firm of Larsen & Co.
text:
Man can effect no great matter by his personal strength, but as he acts in society and conjunction with others.
Received his early education at Darlington Graded Schools. Member of the firm of Larsen & Co.

name: Larsen, George Alexander
motto: Mirth makes him not mad or sobriety sad.
description: Now lives in Charleston, S.C. Received his early education in Charleston public and high schools. Left tenant of signal corps during Senior year. A dealer in sandwiches, pies, soda water, pop, etc.
text:
Mirth makes him not mad or sobriety sad.
Now lives in Charleston, S.C. Received his early education in Charleston public and high schools. Left tenant of signal corps during Senior year. A dealer in sandwiches, pies, soda water, pop, etc.

name: Legerton, Clarence Williams
motto: "For as his own bright image, he survey'd, He fell in love with the fantastic shade."
clubs: Literary Critic and Secretary, Columbian Literary Society
description: And we will read "Jerrytown Military Academy, C.W. Legerton, Commandant of Cadets."
text:
"For as his own bright image, he survey'd, He fell in love with the fantastic shade."
And we will read "Jerrytown Military Academy, C.W. Legerton, Commandant of Cadets."

name: Du Gue, Lewis
motto: "You may depend upon it that he is a good man whose intimate friends are all good, and whose enemies are characters decidedly bad."
description: Will form trusts and consolidate money concerns.
text:
"You may depend upon it that he is a good man whose intimate friends are all good, and whose enemies are characters decidedly bad."
Will form trusts and consolidate money concerns.

name: Vann, Livingston
motto: "You dote on her that cares not for your love."
description: Received his early education in Marlboro Graded Schools. Street railway conductor, attentive and obliging.
text:
"You dote on her that cares not for your love."
Received his early education in Marlboro Graded Schools. Street railway conductor, attentive and obliging.

name: Marvin, Hal W.
motto: It is a difficult task to talk to the purpose and to put life and perspicuity into our discourse.
description: Obtained his early education at Hendersonville Graded School and Carlisle School. Entered College September 1898; a member of Class '02, but left during Senior year on account of health; returned to college September 1902. Will manage a rural telephone system.
text:
It is a difficult task to talk to the purpose and to put life and perspicuity into our discourse.
Obtained his early education at Hendersonville Graded School and Carlisle School. Entered College September 1898; a member of Class '02, but left during Senior year on account of health; returned to college September 1902. Will manage a rural telephone system.

name: Milling, Allie James
motto: Stand not upon the order of going, but go—at once.
description: Early education received in public schools of Greenwood. Was a consistent member of Calhoun Society until abolishment of Craighead; he preferred to be "O. G." than "O. D."; consequently a private in Co. "G"; his love for Geology was great. "Go to A. J. Milling, the Butcher." Fresh meat and mutton.
text:
Stand not upon the order of going, but go—at once.
Early education received in public schools of Greenwood. Was a consistent member of Calhoun Society until abolishment of Craighead; he preferred to be "O. G." than "O. D."; consequently a private in Co. "G"; his love for Geology was great. "Go to A. J. Milling, the Butcher." Fresh meat and mutton.

name: Morrison, G. L.
motto: True greatness is sovereign wisdom, We are never deceived by our virtues.
description: His card will read: G. L. Morrison, A.B., Ph.D., Professor of Hysterics and Hiccoughs.
text:
True greatness is sovereign wisdom, We are never deceived by our virtues.
His card will read: G. L. Morrison, A.B., Ph.D., Professor of Hysterics and Hiccoughs.

name: McCrary, Henry Newton
motto: Oft the hours, From morn till eve have stolen unmarked away, While mute attention hung upon his lips.
description: Received his early education at Pendleton. He lost one year from college but returned and entered Sophomore Class in 1900. In some future war, he will win distinction as commander of an artillery battery.
text:
Oft the hours, From morn till eve have stolen unmarked away, While mute attention hung upon his lips.
Received his early education at Pendleton. He lost one year from college but returned and entered Sophomore Class in 1900. In some future war, he will win distinction as commander of an artillery battery.

name: McSwain, Claude Wightman
motto: He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one.
description: Received his early education at Cross Hill Academy. Entered Freshman Class, 1899. His greatness was in Mathematics. A book agent, with a bright future.
text:
He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one.
Received his early education at Cross Hill Academy. Entered Freshman Class, 1899. His greatness was in Mathematics. A book agent, with a bright future.

name: Newell, Neal Watson
motto: We have all our vices, and the best is he who with the fewest is opprest.
description: Received his early education at Hopewell and Columbia Presbyterian High School. Sarah had a horror of reveille. He will marry Sally and settle down with a mule and forty acres.
text:
We have all our vices, and the best is he who with the fewest is opprest.
Received his early education at Hopewell and Columbia Presbyterian High School. Sarah had a horror of reveille. He will marry Sally and settle down with a mule and forty acres.

name: Norris, George Furman
motto: If circumstances lead me I will find where truth is hid, Though it were hid indeed within the center.
clubs: Treasurer (1901-1902), Secretary, Literary Critic, President, and Class Treasurer
description: Obtained his early education at public schools. Entered College September 1900, as a member of Sophomore Class and pursued the Electrical Course; was a member of the Calhoun Literary Society and held the following offices: Treasurer (1901-1902), Secretary, Literary Critic, President, and Class Treasurer; President of the Epicurean Club; one of Commencement Class orators; won The Chronicle essay medal, 1902, and was Lieutenant in Co. B. He was studious and modest above all. Will gain some notoriety as a pugilist on Coney Island.
text:
If circumstances lead me I will find where truth is hid, Though it were hid indeed within the center.
Obtained his early education at public schools. Entered College September 1900, as a member of Sophomore Class and pursued the Electrical Course; was a member of the Calhoun Literary Society and held the following offices: Treasurer (1901-1902), Secretary, Literary Critic, President, and Class Treasurer; President of the Epicurean Club; one of Commencement Class orators; won The Chronicle essay medal, 1902, and was Lieutenant in Co. B. He was studious and modest above all. Will gain some notoriety as a pugilist on Coney Island.

name: Prioleau, Jacob Ford
motto: In solitary groves he makes his moan. Nor mixed in mirth, in youthful pleasures shares, But sighs when songs and instruments he hears.
description: Received his early education in Beaufort public schools. Entered College September 1898; a member of Calhoun Literary Society; during his Senior year was Lieutenant in Co. A. Electrical Course. Will discover perpetual motion, then go to a home for the feeble-minded.
text:
In solitary groves he makes his moan. Nor mixed in mirth, in youthful pleasures shares, But sighs when songs and instruments he hears.
Received his early education in Beaufort public schools. Entered College September 1898; a member of Calhoun Literary Society; during his Senior year was Lieutenant in Co. A. Electrical Course. Will discover perpetual motion, then go to a home for the feeble-minded.

name: H. R. Pollitzer
motto: O frail estate of human things, Now to our cost your emptiness we know.
description: Attended Beaufort Graded School. Author of "Fizzykunk" and other silly stories.
text:
O frail estate of human things, Now to our cost your emptiness we know.
Attended Beaufort Graded School. Author of "Fizzykunk" and other silly stories.

name: B. Frank Pegues
motto: Who mixed reason with pleasure, and wisdom with mirth.
hometown: Stony Hill Plantation, North Marlboro County
clubs: Secretary, Treasurer, Literary Critic and President, Calhoun Literary Society
description: Obtained his early education at various private and public schools and at the Cheraw Graded Schools. An auctioneer and a country attorney.
text:
Who mixed reason with pleasure, and wisdom with mirth.
Obtained his early education at various private and public schools and at the Cheraw Graded Schools. An auctioneer and a country attorney.

name: Perrin, Thomas Samuel
motto: "And so without tarnish, he bore the grand old name of gentleman."
description: His early education was received in the Abbeville Graded Schools. Entered Freshman Class, 1899: member Palmetto Society, and in it held the following offices: Secretary, Reporting Critic, Vice-President and President; President Tennis Association; member of Glee and Epicurean Clubs; Assistant Manager of Chronicle, 1901-1902, and Manager 1902-1903; member of German and Dramatic Clubs, and Hop Committee of 1902; Poet and then President of Class, 1903; Associate Editor of Oconeean; Captain and Adjutant corps of cadets. A lightning-rod agent, with a ready tongue and pleasant smile.
text:
"And so without tarnish, he bore the grand old name of gentleman."
His early education was received in the Abbeville Graded Schools. Entered Freshman Class, 1899: member Palmetto Society, and in it held the following offices: Secretary, Reporting Critic, Vice-President and President; President Tennis Association; member of Glee and Epicurean Clubs; Assistant Manager of Chronicle, 1901-1902, and Manager 1902-1903; member of German and Dramatic Clubs, and Hop Committee of 1902; Poet and then President of Class, 1903; Associate Editor of Oconeean; Captain and Adjutant corps of cadets. A lightning-rod agent, with a ready tongue and pleasant smile.

name: Quattlebaum, Paul Jones
motto: Conscience is a great ledger book in which all our offences are written and registered.
description: Bum was a great genius (?). We will learn how as a missionary to the Feejee Islands, he was eaten by cannibals (poor cannibals).
text:
Conscience is a great ledger book in which all our offences are written and registered.
Bum was a great genius (?). We will learn how as a missionary to the Feejee Islands, he was eaten by cannibals (poor cannibals).

name: Frank, Kavanaugh Rhodes
motto: Knowledge of our duties is the most useful part of philosophy.
description: Received his early education at Florence and Kershaw Graded Schools. Entered Freshman Class, 1899: a member of the Columbian Literary Society, of which he was Vice-President; Treasurer Y.M.C.A.; Delegate to Southern Students Conference; barrack mail carrier during Senior year; Lieutenant Co. A. Rhodes pursued the Textile Course and was a hard student. He will be seen in the ball-room, heard at the clubs, and noticed at the races; the rest may be imagined.
text:
Knowledge of our duties is the most useful part of philosophy.
Received his early education at Florence and Kershaw Graded Schools. Entered Freshman Class, 1899: a member of the Columbian Literary Society, of which he was Vice-President; Treasurer Y.M.C.A.; Delegate to Southern Students Conference; barrack mail carrier during Senior year; Lieutenant Co. A. Rhodes pursued the Textile Course and was a hard student. He will be seen in the ball-room, heard at the clubs, and noticed at the races; the rest may be imagined.

name: Robertson, James Townes Jr.
motto: Thou has a grim appearance and thy face bears a command in it.
description: He obtained his early education in the public and private schools of Abbeville. He entered Freshman Class in 1899: was an earnest worker in the Palmetto Literary Society, and held the following offices: Sergeant-at-Arms, Reporting Critic, Treasurer and President; played right guard on Freshman and Junior Football Team; was Manager of the Oconeean; class lawyer; 1st Lieutenant D Co.; was a hard student, a true friend and an impartial executor. Will solve the race problem.
text:
Thou has a grim appearance and thy face bears a command in it.
He obtained his early education in the public and private schools of Abbeville. He entered Freshman Class in 1899: was an earnest worker in the Palmetto Literary Society, and held the following offices: Sergeant-at-Arms, Reporting Critic, Treasurer and President; played right guard on Freshman and Junior Football Team; was Manager of the Oconeean; class lawyer; 1st Lieutenant D Co.; was a hard student, a true friend and an impartial executor. Will solve the race problem.

name: Sadler, D. Hope
motto: He was stout of courage, strong of hand. Bold was his heart and restless was his spirit.
clubs: Secretary and Literary Critic of Calhoun Society
description: Will play on the "All American" Football Team and die of a "Charley horse".
text:
He was stout of courage, strong of hand. Bold was his heart and restless was his spirit.
Will play on the "All American" Football Team and die of a "Charley horse".

name: Harry, C. Sahlmann
motto: True as the needle to the pole, the dial to the sun.
description: Local politician; will improve Charleston's public school system.
text:
True as the needle to the pole, the dial to the sun.
Local politician; will improve Charleston's public school system.

name: Tillman, Henry Cumming
motto: He speaks reservedly, but speaks with force, Nor can a word be changed, but for a worse.
description: He will edit the Trenton Astonisher. His editorials will move the whole world to action.
text:
He speaks reservedly, but speaks with force, Nor can a word be changed, but for a worse.
He will edit the Trenton Astonisher. His editorials will move the whole world to action.

name: Wightman, William May
motto: I have sought for rest everywhere and found it nowhere, except in a little corner with a little book.
description: His love was for good books. A Mormon Elder, and a champion of woman's rights.
text:
I have sought for rest everywhere and found it nowhere, except in a little corner with a little book.
His love was for good books. A Mormon Elder, and a champion of woman's rights.

name: Wylie, Joseph Caldwell
motto: Vain show and noise intoxicate the brain, Begin with giddiness and end in pain.
description: Will be head waiter in a down town hotel.
text:
Vain show and noise intoxicate the brain, Begin with giddiness and end in pain.
Will be head waiter in a down town hotel.

name: Whitney, John Barry
motto: By sports like these are all their cares beguiled; The sports of children satisfy the child.
clubs: Clemson Tennis Club (Vice-President, Secretary and Treasurer)
description: Received his early education at the public and private schools of that city. Won the gold medal for best athlete '01; represented the College in several intercollegiate contests. "Eli" is noted for his love of athletic sports. Like "Bouncing Billy," his innocence will keep him in constant trouble.
text:
By sports like these are all their cares beguiled; The sports of children satisfy the child.
Received his early education at the public and private schools of that city. Won the gold medal for best athlete '01; represented the College in several intercollegiate contests. "Eli" is noted for his love of athletic sports. Like "Bouncing Billy," his innocence will keep him in constant trouble.

name: Williams, Robert G., Jr.
motto: Immodest words admit of no defense, For want of decency is want of sense.
description: He will live at Chappells and run the celebrated farm and saw-mill.
text:
Immodest words admit of no defense, For want of decency is want of sense.
He will live at Chappells and run the celebrated farm and saw-mill.

name: Young, Thomas Benton
motto: "If little labor, little are our gains; Man's fortunes are according to his pains."
clubs: Reporting Critic, Prosecuting Critic, Recording Secretary, Treasurer, Columbian Literary Society
Captain and Quartermaster, Corps of Cadets, '02-'03
description: Obtained his early education at the public schools at Ebenezer. Entered Sub-Freshman Class, September, 1898. Will teach a country school and grow tobacco.
text:
"If little labor, little are our gains; Man's fortunes are according to his pains."
Obtained his early education at the public schools at Ebenezer. Entered Sub-Freshman Class, September, 1898. Will teach a country school and grow tobacco.

name: Wyse, Joseph H.
motto: "No man so potent, breathes upon the ground, But I will beard him."
description: His early education was received at Prosperity Graded School and Columbia High School. He entered the Freshman Class in 1899, and became closely identified with all college work; was a member of the Columbian Literary Society, holding the following offices: Chaplain, Sergeant-at-Arms, Secretary, Literary and Reporting Critics, and President; won Columbian Society's orator's medal, 1903; one of the three class orators for Commencement; member Track Team, 1900; member of 3x4 Tennis Club; Manager Track Team, 1901 and 1902; Manager Field Day, 1901; Manager Football Team, 1902 and 1903; Manager, Secretary, and Treasurer of Baseball Team, 1902; Manager Nick Oyster Club, 1901; Assistant Manager of The Oconeean; member of Finance Committee of Hop, 1902; member of Athletic Council, and Vice-President General Athletic Association, 1903; right tenant of the Signal Corps. Joe's shrewdness in money matters and other things won him quite a reputation at Clemson. Prophecy: Will attempt a political life, but meeting with defeat, will retire as a green grocer.
text:
"No man so potent, breathes upon the ground, But I will beard him."
His early education was received at Prosperity Graded School and Columbia High School. He entered the Freshman Class in 1899, and became closely identified with all college work; was a member of the Columbian Literary Society, holding the following offices: Chaplain, Sergeant-at-Arms, Secretary, Literary and Reporting Critics, and President; won Columbian Society's orator's medal, 1903; one of the three class orators for Commencement; member Track Team, 1900; member of 3x4 Tennis Club; Manager Track Team, 1901 and 1902; Manager Field Day, 1901; Manager Football Team, 1902 and 1903; Manager, Secretary, and Treasurer of Baseball Team, 1902; Manager Nick Oyster Club, 1901; Assistant Manager of The Oconeean; member of Finance Committee of Hop, 1902; member of Athletic Council, and Vice-President General Athletic Association, 1903; right tenant of the Signal Corps. Joe's shrewdness in money matters and other things won him quite a reputation at Clemson. Prophecy: Will attempt a political life, but meeting with defeat, will retire as a green grocer.
